methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Living Carbon is a Series A biotech company that has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public-market comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in biotech and health—including Series B companies such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-Progression anchors to the $36M already raised and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $72M. Peer-Median triangulation across the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $805M, with a range from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.6x across the three public peers) cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target.

The triangulated range spans $61M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $438M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Living Carbon's revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts, and timing of next funding event. The stage-progression method and peer-median method produce meaningfully different results; weighting toward the lower end of the peer distribution may be warranted absent evidence of outsized clinical or commercial progress relative to Series B comparables.
